regions: Other Power Regions (New England, South, West, Canada)

dependencies: Nuclear fuel obtained predominantly through long-term contracts for uranium concentrates, conversion services, enrichment services, and fabrication services, including contracts sourced from Russia
Approximately 45% of uranium concentrate requirements from 2025 through 2029 are supplied by three suppliers
Natural gas procured through long-term and short-term contracts, as well as spot-market purchases
risks: Supply markets for nuclear fuel, natural gas, and oil are subject to price fluctuations, availability restrictions, tariffs, counterparty default, and geopolitical risk, including the ongoing Russia and Ukraine conflict
United States, United Kingdom, European Union, and Canadian sanctions impacting exports and imports of Russian nuclear fuel
Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act bans import of low-enriched uranium produced in Russia or by Russian entities, absent a waiver from the DOE
Non-performance by nuclear fuel suppliers could have a material adverse impact on consolidated financial statements
Geopolitical developments, including Russia-Ukraine conflict and sanctions, have potential to impact delivery from multiple suppliers in the international uranium processing industry

regulatory_risks: FERC jurisdiction over wholesale sales of electricity and transmission in interstate commerce, including authority to suspend market-based rates
NRC regulations covering operations, maintenance, emergency planning, security, environmental and radiological aspects of nuclear generating stations
NRC may modify, suspend, or revoke operating licenses and impose violations and/or civil penalties for failure to comply
Changes in NRC requirements may require substantial increase in capital expenditures and/or operating costs for nuclear generating facilities
Federal and state environmental legislation and regulation by local, state and federal authorities affecting air and water emissions, hazardous and solid waste, and impacts on species and habitats
Clean Air Act regulations including EPA GHG standards for existing coal, new natural gas-fired power plants, and existing oil/gas steam generators
Clean Water Act Section 316(b) requirements for cooling water intake structures
CERCLA and RCRA liability for cleanup costs of hazardous substances at sites
State mandates requiring increasingly stringent policies for reduction of GHG emissions over time
Renewable and clean energy procurement requirements in 35 states and District of Columbia
FERC licensing requirements for hydroelectric facilities
NERC mandatory reliability standards protecting bulk power system against cyber and physical security breaches
State-sponsored programs (ZECs, CMCs) requiring refund or pass through of nuclear PTC
Potential repeal or significant reduction of nuclear PTC could have material impact on financial performance
Additional guidance from U.S. Treasury and IRS on nuclear PTC may materially impact benefits received
Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) federal tax credits subject to legislative and regulatory changes
NRC regulations requiring demonstration of reasonable assurance that funds will be available for nuclear decommissioning
State public utility commission regulations affecting former PECO and STP units decommissioning funding
Environmental agency actions could subject company to enforcement actions, capital expenditures, operating costs, civil penalties and third-party claims
Price-Anderson Act liability limits and mandatory participation in financial protection pool for nuclear incidents
Nuclear Waste Policy Act requiring DOE disposal of spent nuclear fuel (SNF), with DOE failure to meet obligations creating storage cost liabilities

sanction_risks: United States, United Kingdom, European Union, and Canadian sanctions against Russia impacting nuclear fuel exports and imports
Prohibiting Russian Uranium Imports Act banning import of low-enriched uranium from Russia
Potential for Russia to limit fuel deliveries in response to sanctions
Ongoing Russia and Ukraine conflict yielding sanctions and legislation impacting nuclear fuel supply
Geopolitical issues including conflicts in Ukraine and Middle East, and tensions between U.S. and China, may motivate cyber-attacks impacting U.S. energy sector
